fields.remarks:
<format type="text/markdown"><![CDATA[
## Remarks

`SKFourByteTag` represents a four-character code (FourCC) packed into a 32-bit unsigned integer, where the first character occupies the most-significant byte. This format is used throughout OpenType and Skia for identifying font tables, variation axes, and other tagged resources.

You can construct an `SKFourByteTag` from four characters (e.g., `new SKFourByteTag('w','g','h','t')`), from a raw `uint`, or by parsing a string with `SKFourByteTag.Parse`. The implicit conversions to and from `uint` make it interoperable with raw tag values.

This struct is a value type and does not require disposal.

## Examples

```csharp
var wght = SKFourByteTag.Parse("wght");
var ital = new SKFourByteTag('i', 't', 'a', 'l');
Console.WriteLine(wght.ToString()); // "wght"
```
]]></format>
fields.summary: Represents a four-byte tag (FourCC) used to identify OpenType font tables, variation axes, and other resources.

params.value: The raw 32-bit unsigned integer value of the tag.

fields.returns: An <see cref="T:SkiaSharp.SKFourByteTag" /> parsed from the string. If <paramref name="tag" /> is <see langword="null" /> or empty, returns a tag with the value zero. Strings shorter than four characters are padded with spaces; longer strings are truncated.

params.tag: The string to parse. Only the first four characters are used; shorter strings are padded with spaces.

fields.summary: Parses a string into a <see cref="T:SkiaSharp.SKFourByteTag" />.

fields.returns: A four-character string representation of the tag.
fields.summary: Returns the four-character string representation of this tag.
